title: Little Cottage Loft Bed
summary: A raised twin loft bed styled as a cottage — open rail walls with a triangular beadboard-paneled gable end, distinct from this catalog's other elevated kids beds.
description:
This loft bed sits between the two other elevated kids' beds in this catalog: unlike the playhouse loft bed's flat plywood panel walls, this design uses open 2x2 rail-and-baluster walls for most of its perimeter, reserving solid material for just one triangular gable end — filled with beadboard paneling rather than the clubhouse bed's individual angle-cut boards. That gable end is the plan's one genuinely fussy assembly: frame the triangle first, then cut the beadboard panel to the frame's actual finished shape rather than a calculated one, since beadboard sheet stock rarely lines up perfectly with a hand-framed angle.

Build the loft platform as a complete, square, standalone deck on four corner posts before adding a single rail — a raised bed's rails only make sense relative to a deck that's already the right size and already level. The rails and balusters go up next, spaced no more than 4" apart at any point a child could pass through, which is the safety spec this plan treats as non-negotiable rather than decorative.

The ladder attaches at a shallow enough angle to climb safely and must be fastened directly to the bed frame, never just leaned in place. **This is an elevated sleeping surface for a child** — verify rail height, baluster spacing, and guardrail coverage on all open sides against current crib/bunk-bed safety standards before use, and anchor the whole structure to wall studs if it sits against a wall.

tools.note: Cutting the beadboard gable panel to the framed triangle's actual shape.

cutList.note: Spaced no more than 4" apart, verified against current guardrail safety standards.
Two angled top members, one bottom.
Cut to the framed triangle's actual finished shape, not a calculated one.

steps.title: Build the platform deck
steps.body: Assemble the platform frame and joists from 2x6s, then attach the plywood deck on top. Build this as a complete, square, level unit before adding posts or rails.

steps.title: Attach the corner posts
steps.body: Attach the four corner posts to the underside of the platform frame with lag bolts, checking plumb on all four posts before final tightening.

steps.title: Build and attach the rails
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom rails to the posts on all open sides, then space and attach the balusters no more than 4" apart. Verify spacing against current guardrail safety standards before continuing.

steps.title: Build the gable end
steps.body: Frame the triangular gable end at one end of the bed. Cut the beadboard panel to the frame's actual finished shape with a jigsaw, then attach with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the ladder
steps.body: Build the ladder from the rails and rungs, then fasten it directly and securely to the bed frame at a safe climbing angle — never leaned in place unattached.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces smooth, paying extra attention to rail and baluster edges. Anchor the structure to wall studs if placed against a wall. Prime and paint.

unresolvedImages.alt: Cottage-style loft bed with rail walls and a beadboard gable end
